title: grastidium cyclolobum
intro: Grastidium cyclolobum, also known as the "tall golden-pea", is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae and is native to Australia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its seeds which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The seeds have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Grastidium cyclolobum seeds can be prepared into herbal remedies through decoction or infusion, and are also used in traditional medicine preparations such as powders and tinctures.
